clinical_history: This ECG, from a young adult man with progressive weakness and dyspnea is most consistent with which ONE of the following diagnoses?
diagnosis: The ECG shows sinus rhythm with right axis deviation. The right-mid precordial R waves are very prominent, with a drop-off in voltage laterally. The P waves are notched raising consideration of left atrial abnormality. The T waves are inverted in the right precordial leads. While the findings raise consideration of right ventricular hypertrophy, they do not account for the P waves (one might expect RAA). In the context of the neuromuscular history in a young adult male, these findings are most consistent with Duchenne muscular dystrophy. The tall right precordial R waves are attributable to posterior-basal fibrosis. Additional features often include prominent Q waves laterally, producing a pseudo-infarction pattern (posterolateral MI mimic), and sometimes concomitant atrial arrhythmias and other conduction disturbances.
